skill.name: remotion-render
skill.description: Render videos from React/Remotion component code via inference.sh. Pass TSX code, get MP4. Supports all Remotion APIs: useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, Sequence. Configurable resolution, FPS, duration, codec. Use for: programmatic video generation, animated graphics, motion design, data-driven videos, React animations to video. Triggers: remotion, render video from code, tsx to video, react video, programmatic video, remotion render, code to video, animated video, motion graphics code, react animation video
skill.summary: Render videos from React/Remotion component code via inference.sh
skill.author: skills-shell

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 41 potential issues, but evaluation reveals all HIGH and MEDIUM findings are false positives. The scanner misidentified markdown code fences as Ruby backtick execution and YAML frontmatter as cryptographic algorithms. LOW findings are legitimate hardcoded URLs to the inference.sh service. This is a legitimate video rendering skill that wraps the belt CLI tool to call inference.sh's Remotion rendering API. Risk factors are confined to the expected belt tool usage and network calls required for core functionality.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Misidentified Cryptographic Pattern
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 3 and 35. Evaluation reveals these are false positives: line 3 is YAML frontmatter describing the skill, and line 35 is a markdown table header. No cryptographic algorithms exist in this file.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains YAML frontmatter with a description field, and line 35 is a markdown table separator. No cryptographic code is present in either location.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Misidentified Shell Execution Pattern
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at 32 locations. Evaluation reveals these are false positives - the scanner misidentified markdown code fences (```bash) and inline code markers as Ruby backtick execution. All flagged locations contain legitimate documentation examples showing belt CLI usage.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations contain markdown code fences or inline code examples for the belt CLI tool. No Ruby backtick execution exists - the scanner pattern-matched markdown syntax incorrectly.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs to inference.sh Service
low_findings.description: Six hardcoded URLs to inference.sh domain are present. These are legitimate URLs to the backend service required for video rendering functionality. Not malicious.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to inference.sh service homepage, image hosting, documentation, and GitHub raw content. All are legitimate service endpoints required for the skill to function.

content.value_statement: Developers need to create programmatic videos from React components but lack tooling to render TSX code to MP4. This skill enables rendering videos from Remotion/React code via the inference.sh belt CLI, supporting animations, motion graphics, and data-driven videos.
actual_capabilities: Render MP4 videos from React/Remotion TSX component code
Support all Remotion APIs: useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, Sequence
Configure video output: resolution, FPS, duration, codec
Pass dynamic props to components for data-driven videos
Create animations with opacity, scale, and sequence timing
limitations: Requires inference.sh account and belt CLI installation
Video rendering happens on remote inference.sh servers
Large videos may take significant time to render

use_cases.title: Motion graphics automation
use_cases.description: Create animated title sequences, data visualizations, and promotional videos from React code. Perfect for content creators who want programmatic video generation.

use_cases.title: Data-driven video generation
use_cases.description: Render videos that incorporate dynamic data as props. Generate personalized videos at scale for marketing, reports, or social media.

use_cases.title: React animation prototyping
use_cases.description: Quickly test and preview Remotion animations by rendering directly from code. Iterate on animation timing and effects without a full Remotion project setup.

prompt_templates.title: Simple text animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Render a video with a white text 'Hello World' centered on a black background that fades in over 30 frames.

prompt_templates.title: Animated counter
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a video showing a counter from 0 to 100 percent that counts up over 5 seconds at 60 FPS. Use a white circular progress indicator on a black background.

prompt_templates.title: Spring animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Render a video with a white rounded square that bounces with spring physics on a purple background. The bounce should last 2 seconds at 60 FPS.

prompt_templates.title: Sequenced multi-element animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a video with three text elements ('First', 'Second', 'Third') that fade in sequentially. Each element appears for 1 second with 0.5 second gaps between them. Total duration 4 seconds.

output_examples.input: Render a video with centered white text 'Welcome' on a dark blue background that fades in smoothly.
output_examples.output: A rendered MP4 video file (1920x1080, 30 FPS, 2 seconds) showing white 'Welcome' text fading in on a dark blue background.

output_examples.input: Create a progress animation that counts from 0 to 100 over 5 seconds with a loading indicator.
output_examples.output: A rendered MP4 video showing a counting percentage with a 'Loading...' label, counting up smoothly on a black background.

best_practices: Start with simple animations and test rendering before adding complex effects
Use appropriate FPS settings: 30 for standard video, 60 for smooth animations
Keep component code focused on the visual output; pass dynamic data as props
anti_patterns: Do not include external API calls or network requests inside the TSX component code
Avoid very long duration videos as they increase rendering time significantly
Do not use this for real-time video streaming; it is designed for batch video generation

faq.question: What is Remotion?
faq.answer: Remotion is a framework for creating videos using React. It allows you to build video components using React's component model and APIs.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install the belt CLI tool from inference.sh. Run: npx skills add belt-sh/cli

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill outputs MP4 videos. You can configure resolution, FPS, duration, and codec parameters.

faq.question: Can I use external images or assets?
faq.answer: The skill supports importing from 'remotion' and 'react' packages. For external assets, you would need to provide them as base64 or use available Img/Video components.

faq.question: How long does rendering take?
faq.answer: Rendering time depends on video complexity, duration, and resolution. Simple 2-3 second videos typically render in under a minute.

faq.question: Is there a size limit for videos?
faq.answer: There may be limits based on inference.sh service quotas. For very long videos, consider breaking them into shorter segments.
